examples:
Command Example
!aws-logs-filter-log-events logGroupName=test
Context Example
Human Readable Output
6. Get a list of log groups
Lists the specified log groups. You can list all your log groups or filter the results by prefix. The results are ASCII-sorted by log group name.
Base Command
aws-logs-describe-log-groups

!aws-logs-filter-log-events logGroupName=test
Context Example
Human Readable Output
6. Get a list of log groups
Lists the specified log groups. You can list all your log groups or filter the results by prefix. The results are ASCII-sorted by log group name.
Base Command
aws-logs-describe-log-groups
Input
name: aws-logs-describe-log-groups

outputs.description: The name of the log group. AWS.CloudWatchLogs.LogGroups.CreationTime The creation time of the log group, expressed as the number of milliseconds after Jan 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC.
outputs.name: AWS.CloudWatchLogs.LogGroups.LogGroupName

outputs.description: The number of days to retain the log events in the specified log group. AWS.CloudWatchLogs.LogGroups.MetricFilterCount The number of metric filters.
outputs.name: AWS.CloudWatchLogs.LogGroups.RetentionInDays

outputs.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the log group. AWS.CloudWatchLogs.LogGroups.StoredBytes The number of bytes stored.
outputs.name: AWS.CloudWatchLogs.LogGroups.Arn

outputs.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the CMK to use when encrypting log data.
outputs.name: AWS.CloudWatchLogs.LogGroups.KmsKeyId
